hero.title: Practice real engineering work before your first job.
hero.subtitle: IndustrCons IRE-3 puts you inside realistic construction projects — drawings, BOQs, site logs, and deadlines — so you build the judgment employers actually hire for.

why.title: Textbooks teach theory. Sites teach judgment.
why.subtitle: Every internship is built from a real workflow used on real projects — you don't watch a lecture, you make the call.
why.f1body: Interpret actual-format drawings, BOQs, RFIs and method statements — the paperwork your first job runs on.
why.f2body: Every submission returns the correct answer, the engineering reasoning behind it, and the mistake most beginners make.
why.f3body: You're assigned by a manager, inside a company, on a project with a budget and a deadline — pressure included.
why.f4body: Finish with a verifiable certificate and a ready-to-post LinkedIn summary of exactly what you practiced.

catalogPreview.title: Choose the seat you want at the table.

companies.title: You're hired by a company, not a chapter.
companies.subtitle: Fictional firms, built with real organizational depth — mission, culture, live projects — inspired by how leading international contractors operate.
companies.inspirationBody: IRE-3's scenarios are inspired by practices at firms such as AECOM, Bechtel, Skanska, Turner Construction, VINCI Construction, STRABAG, Fluor, Jacobs and Balfour Beatty. IndustrCons is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or partnered with these companies; they are referenced only as examples of international engineering practice.

stats.title: Built for depth, not just completion.

cta.title: Your first site assignment is waiting.
cta.subtitle: Free to start. No credit card. Certificate on completion.

dashboard.welcomeSub: Here's where your internship stands today.
dashboard.noCurrent: You haven't started an internship yet.
dashboard.noActivity: No activity yet — start a task to see it here.

catalog.title: Internship Catalog
catalog.subtitle: 15 roles across the AEC project lifecycle. Start with one, or map a career path across several.

certificate.title: Certificate of Completion
certificate.completed: has successfully completed the virtual internship
certificate.nameLabel: Name to print on your credential
certificate.nameHint: This should be your own name — it will be printed on the credential and shown if you share it on LinkedIn. You can change it anytime; changes appear below instantly.
certificate.linkedinCaptionHint: This text will be copied and LinkedIn will open. When writing your post, type @IndustrCons and @Elvin Asgarov and select them from the list so they're properly tagged.
certificate.notReadyText: Finish the internship's tasks, case study, and quiz first — then come back to generate this certificate.
